items.title: Iran says peace talks would be 'unreasonable' as Israel expands Lebanon strikes — Reuters
items.summary: Sporadic fighting continued in the Middle East, including in Lebanon, killing hundreds of people and drawing a threat of retaliation from Iran, Reuters reported on Thursday.

items.title: NZD/USD holds fragile footing as USD demand returns amid geopolitical noise
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is trading with a cautious tone around the 0.5830 region on Thursday, as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) regains some traction following shifting geopolitical headlines.

items.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Forms shooting-star, drops below 112.00 as bears lurk
items.summary: The Australian Dollar extended its gains versus the Japanese Yen, driven by an improvement in risk appetite amid the two-week pause in the Middle East conflict between the US and Iran.

items.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Rallies above 0.5800, eyes on 200-day SMA
items.summary: The NZD/USD rallied sharply on Wednesday, boosted by a double whammy. The de-escalation of the Middle East conflict, and hawkish remarks by the RBNZ Governor, Anna Breman, post the bank’s monetary policy meeting.

items.title: Singapore: Energy buffers seen limiting near‑term risks – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lloyd Chan argues Singapore’s energy system and fiscal strength materially limit near‑term tail risks from Middle East tensions.

items.title: Forex Today: FOMC Minutes reinforce 'higher-for-longer' as markets digest the ceasefire
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) holds firm near 99.10, supported by safe-haven demand late in the American session with expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) will remain cautious on easing.

items.title: US: Demand still dominant but moderating – BNP Paribas
items.summary: BNP Paribas analysts assess that in the United States (USD), demand remains the main driver of inflation, though its contribution has eased from post‑Covid peaks.

items.title: DXY bounces from fresh lows as ceasefire cracks widen on all sides
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) continued to grind lower during the front half of the US trading session on Wednesday, falling roughly 1% from the prior session's close near 100.00 to tag a low around 98.50 as the US-Iran ceasefire announcement triggered a broad wave of risk-on selling in the US Dollar.

items.title: Hungary: Inflation set to reaccelerate – ING
items.summary: ING economists Peter Virovacz and Zoltán Homolya note that Hungarian inflation in March 2026 surprised on the downside versus expectations but remained above February’s decade low.

items.title: WTI slides sharply after US-Iran ceasefire removes war premium
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil trades sharply lower on Wednesday, falling more than 10% after a temporary ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran reduced the geopolitical risk premium embedded in prices.

items.title: Fed Minutes to offer insights into March hold decision amid hawkish outlook
items.summary: The Federal Reserve (Fed) will publish its Minutes from the March 18 meeting on Wednesday. The release should be less about the decision itself and more about the officials’ “no rush to cut” narrative.

items.title: Brent: Elevated prices after ceasefire relief – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen notes that Brent prices dropped by USD 15 on news of a two‑week U.S.–Iran ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but still trade near USD 95, well above pre‑war levels.
